name: Virtual Data Warehouse
description: A decision-support environment using metadata to provide unified access to distributed data sources without physical data consolidation, introduced October 2, 1995.
lifecycle_note: The virtual data warehouse concept evolved into data virtualization and, more recently, data fabric architectures. The specific Intersolv product was discontinued after Merant/Micro Focus restructuring.

name: DataDirect SmartData
description: Intelligent metadata layer implemented as an ODBC driver that shields users from SQL complexity and creates virtual databases (SmartSets) over physical data sources.
lifecycle_note: Specific SmartData branding discontinued post-merger; metadata management capabilities absorbed into subsequent DataDirect products.

name: DataDirect Explorer
description: Desktop query, analysis, and reporting tool providing Query Builder, Chart Builder, Report Builder, Form Builder, and Job Scheduler components.
lifecycle_note: Explorer desktop product discontinued following Merant restructuring; capabilities partially absorbed into successor BI tooling.

name: DataDirect ODBC Pack
description: Set of ODBC drivers supporting more than 35 databases on eight operating systems, connecting any ODBC-compliant application to personal, relational, or legacy databases.
lifecycle_note: ODBC driver product line continues under Progress DataDirect brand, supporting modern databases and cloud sources.

name: DataDirect SequeLink
description: Server-based database-independent middleware for client-server connectivity, enabling heterogeneous joins, cursor management, and legacy integration without performance-degrading gateways.
lifecycle_note: SequeLink product was eventually discontinued after Intersolv/Merant transitions; server-side connectivity approach supplanted by modern middleware.
successor_technology: JDBC/ODBC middleware; modern data integration platforms

name: ODBC (Open Database Connectivity)
description: Standard API for accessing database management systems, allowing applications to connect to any ODBC-compliant data source regardless of database vendor.
lifecycle_note: ODBC remains an active and widely supported standard for database connectivity, supplemented by JDBC and newer APIs.

name: SQL (Structured Query Language)
description: Standard language for relational database queries; the Virtual Data Warehouse was designed to shield business users from writing SQL directly.
lifecycle_note: SQL remains the dominant query language for relational databases; standardized through ANSI/ISO; extended with window functions, JSON support, and other features.

name: RDBMS (Relational Database Management System)
description: Class of database systems based on relational model; referenced as the primary foundation for physical data warehouses.
lifecycle_note: RDBMSs remain foundational; augmented by columnar stores (Redshift, BigQuery, Snowflake) optimized for analytical workloads.
successor_technology: Columnar / analytical RDBMS; cloud data warehouses

name: Multidimensional Database
description: Proprietary database optimized for OLAP; referenced as alternative to RDBMS for data warehousing (e.g., Cognos Powerplay).
lifecycle_note: Dedicated MDDBs largely replaced by columnar databases with OLAP extensions; concept persists in cloud OLAP engines.

name: Metadata Management
description: Practice of cataloging and managing data about data; central to the Virtual Data Warehouse's SmartData semantic layer approach.
lifecycle_note: Metadata management has grown in importance; modern data catalogs (Apache Atlas, Alation, Collibra) and data governance platforms are direct successors.

name: Microsoft OLE 2.0
description: Object Linking and Embedding standard for embedding objects (e.g., Word documents) within applications; used by Explorer Report Builder.
lifecycle_note: OLE 2.0 succeeded by COM, then .NET, then modern web embedding standards. Still supported in Windows but no longer a primary integration approach.

name: IBM DB2
description: IBM relational database management system; referenced as a snapshot source in the Intelligent Snapshot feature.
lifecycle_note: IBM Db2 remains an active product; modernized with cloud and containerized deployments.

name: Cognos Impromptu
description: Query and reporting tool from Cognos Corp., a direct competitor to DataDirect Explorer; noted for stronger enterprise metadata dissemination features.
lifecycle_note: Cognos Impromptu discontinued after IBM acquisition; capabilities merged into IBM Cognos BI suite.

name: Business Objects BusinessObjects
description: BI platform from Business Objects; a direct competitor to the Virtual Data Warehouse; noted for strong semantic layer but tool-specific metadata.
lifecycle_note: BusinessObjects survived acquisition by SAP; continues as SAP BusinessObjects BI platform.

name: Business Objects Mercury
description: Decision-support system combining intelligent metadata with desktop microcubes for a virtual multidimensional environment; announced for early 1996 release.
lifecycle_note: Mercury was released but later discontinued; concept of microcubes evolved into local OLAP/in-memory analytics.
